questions.question: What is the largest ocean?
questions.explanation: The Pacific Ocean covers an area of approximately 155 million square kilometers.

questions.question: How many continents are there?
questions.explanation: The 7 continents are Africa, Antarctica, Asia, Australia, Europe, North America, and South America.

questions.question: What is the smallest continent?
questions.explanation: Australia is the smallest of the 7 continents, covering an area of approximately 8.5 million square kilometers.

questions.question: How many islands are there on Earth?
questions.explanation: There are lakhs of islands on the planet, with varying sizes.

questions.question: What is the Indian Ocean Tsunami Warning System?
questions.correct_answer: A system to detect and warn about tsunamis in the Indian Ocean
questions.explanation: Many countries collaborate in this early warning system to protect lives and property.

questions.question: Which continent has a very cold climate and harsh environment?
questions.explanation: India established its first scientific base station, 'Dakshin Gangotri', on Antarctica in 1983.

questions.question: What is the role of oceans in the Earth's water cycle?
questions.explanation: Oceans play a crucial part in the Earth's water cycle, sending rain to the continents.

questions.question: How many states would it take to equal the size of Greenland?
questions.explanation: Greenland is the largest island in the world, and its size is equivalent to the combined area of 10 large Indian states.

questions.question: How many islands does India have?
questions.explanation: India has more than 1,300 small islands, including Andaman and Nicobar Islands and Lakshadweep Islands.

questions.question: What is the name of the Indian scientific base station in Antarctica?
questions.explanation: The Dakshin Gangotri base station was established by India on Antarctica in 1983.

important_notes.title: Continents and Oceans
important_notes.content: There are 7 continents: Africa, Antarctica, Asia, Australia, Europe, North America, and South America. The 5 oceans are Pacific, Atlantic, Indian, Southern, and Arctic.

important_notes.title: Water Distribution
important_notes.content: Oceans cover most of the Earth's surface, with the Pacific Ocean being the largest. The distribution of water is not equal between the Northern and Southern Hemispheres.

important_notes.title: Islands
important_notes.content: There are lakhs of islands on the planet, ranging from small to large in size. Some notable island groups include Andaman and Nicobar Islands and Lakshadweep Islands.

important_notes.title: Tsunami Warning System
important_notes.content: The Indian Ocean Tsunami Warning System is an early warning system that detects and warns about tsunamis in the Indian Ocean, protecting lives and property.

important_notes.title: Antarctica Research
important_notes.content: India has been conducting research on Antarctica since 1981, focusing on climate evolution and environmental studies. The Dakshin Gangotri base station was established in 1983.

important_notes.title: Oceans and Life
important_notes.content: Oceans play a vital role in the Earth's water cycle, sending rain to continents, and support most aspects of life on Earth.

important_notes.title: Continents and Their Sizes
important_notes.content: The 7 continents have varying sizes, with Australia being the smallest and Asia being the largest. The diagram shows their relative sizes.

important_notes.title: Ocean Boundaries
important_notes.content: The boundaries between oceans are not strictly defined but are generally agreed upon by geographers and cartographers.

important_notes.title: Disaster Management
important_notes.content: India has a National Disaster Management Authority to handle all kinds of disasters, including tsunamis and other natural calamities.

important_notes.title: Earth's Water Cycle
important_notes.content: The Earth's water cycle is the continuous process by which water circulates between the oceans, atmosphere, and land. Oceans play a crucial role in this cycle.

facts.fact: The Pacific Ocean covers an area of approximately 155 million square kilometers.
There are lakhs of islands on the planet, with varying sizes.
Greenland is the largest island in the world, and its size is equivalent to the combined area of 10 large Indian states.
India has more than 1,300 small islands, including Andaman and Nicobar Islands and Lakshadweep Islands.
The Dakshin Gangotri base station was established by India on Antarctica in 1983.
Oceans cover most of the Earth's surface, with the Pacific Ocean being the largest.
There are 7 continents: Africa, Antarctica, Asia, Australia, Europe, North America, and South America.
The Indian Ocean Tsunami Warning System is an early warning system that detects and warns about tsunamis in the Indian Ocean.
India has been conducting research on Antarctica since 1981, focusing on climate evolution and environmental studies.
Oceans play a vital role in the Earth's water cycle, sending rain to continents, and support most aspects of life on Earth.
